title: SQLite MCP via local uv with a ~/ db-path → BLOCK (broad path + unverified local source)

origin.description: Public documentation config from modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived (src/sqlite/README.md, uv variant). Verbatim except normalization to a valid JSON root. The launcher is a local `uv --directory <repo path> run mcp-server-sqlite --db-path ~/test.db`; the ~/test.db argument is upstream's own example path.

humanExpectation.rationale: Two findings fire. (1) files.broad-path (critical blocker, S2): the --db-path argument `~/test.db` starts with the broad home root `~/`, granting access at the user's home directory → BLOCK. (2) exec.unverified-local-source (REVIEW-class, S2): `uv` is a local runtime (not a recognized package runner — only uvx is) running a local module CallLint never inspects (ADR 0011 Direction 2). The blocker dominates, so the verdict is BLOCK. This is the first real case to exercise the new exec.unverified-local-source finding alongside a blocker, and a real broad-path BLOCK that is NOT a docker bind mount (contrast C023) and NOT an npx filesystem server (contrast C020).
knownLimitations: `uv run` here launches a packaged module from a local checkout; CallLint flags the source as unverified (it never reads the module) and the home-rooted db path as broad. A workspace-scoped db path would drop the blocker, leaving only the REVIEW-class local-source finding.
